title: Crispy Smoky Turkish Chicken Kebabs
description: Bitter, crunchy solodinner Turkish dish featuring peach juice and a smoky finish, Whole30friendly and bursting with aromatic spices.
instructions: Trim any excess fat from the chicken thighs and cut them into 1.5‑inch cubes.

In a medium bowl whisk together peach juice, olive oil, smoked paprika, ground cumin, ground coriander, salt, black pepper, minced garlic, lemon zest, lemon juice, and liquid smoke until well blended.
Add the chicken cubes to the bowl, tossing to coat each piece completely. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 20 minutes, up to 2 hours.
Soak wooden skewers in warm water for 30 minutes to prevent burning.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium‑high heat (~400 °F/200 °C). Lightly oil the grates with a paper towel dipped in oil if using an outdoor grill.
Thread the marinated chicken onto the soaked skewers, leaving a small gap between pieces for even cooking.
Place the skewers on the grill and cook 4–5 minutes on the first side until a light char forms.
Flip the skewers, grill another 4–5 minutes, brushing with a little of the reserved peach‑marinade for extra flavor and color.
Use a meat thermometer to confirm the internal temperature has reached 165 °F (74 °C). Remove the kebabs and let them rest on a plate for 5 minutes.
Serve hot, optionally garnishing with a sprinkle of chopped fresh parsley or a drizzle of extra peach juice.
